summary: Dependency-confusion proof-of-concept beacon to loopback registry
details: Package exports a single function `command()` that, when explicitly called by a consumer, runs `curl -X PUT http://0.0.0.0:4873/-/user/org.couchdb.user:vertfinal` with hardcoded credentials (`vertfinal` / `vertfinal123`) against a local Verdaccio registry instance. The destination is loopback (0.0.0.0:4873), so no data leaves the installer's machine, and no installer secrets, environment variables, or credentials are read. There is no install/postinstall lifecycle hook — the code only fires when a caller invokes `require('internallib_v557').command()`. Combined with the `internallib_v557` name and token-style strings (`VERT16X_TOKEN`, `FULL_TOKEN:`), this matches the canonical dependency-confusion research / proof-of-concept shape rather than a functional supply-chain attack. The package has no legitimate utility, but installing or importing it does not harm the installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
